deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explicitly explains why it is withholding direct answers or examples—why guided discovery supports learning better than being given a model. It enforces constraints (e.g., 'I won't write the forum post for you') but never articulates the pedagogical rationale behind that stance.

In Transcript 3, the student makes clear progress from Turn 8 to Turn 10 (moving from vague bullets to a structured, evidence-based bullet). The tutor affirms the improvement but does not prompt the student to reflect on what changed in their thinking or why the revised approach worked better.

grade.overview: The tutor performs strongly across all three transcripts. It consistently refuses to do the student's work, uses Socratic questioning to guide discovery, builds on student responses, and redirects off-task requests appropriately. Responses are concise and well-structured. Two deductions apply: the tutor never explains the pedagogical rationale for its constraints (1.3.A.a), and in Transcript 3 it misses a clear opportunity to prompt reflection after the student's notable improvement from Turn 8 to Turn 10 (1.3.C.a). No other rubric criteria are triggered.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: The tutor never gives submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and consistently lets the student generate key ideas before confirming or refining them. Full marks. 1.2: The tutor builds on each student's partial answers across all three conversations and asks diagnostic questions before providing explanations. Full marks. 1.3: The tutor never explains why it withholds direct answers—it simply enforces the constraint without articulating the learning rationale (1.3.A.a, -2). In Transcript 3, after the student produces a clearly improved bullet in Turn 10, the tutor moves forward without asking the student to reflect on what made the revision better (1.3.C.a, -2). 1.3.B is not triggered because the tutor gives specific, actionable process feedback throughout (e.g., the 4-part content formula, the leading/engaged/missing tests). Dialogue quality and communication quality are both clean: no spiraling, no assignment drift, responses are appropriately bite-sized, and tone is consistently supportive without being evaluative.
